rusty_dagger.name: a rusty dagger
rusty_dagger.description: A pitted iron dagger, more rust than blade, but it still holds an edge.

short_sword.name: a short sword
short_sword.description: A well-balanced soldier's blade with a leather-wrapped grip.

oak_staff.name: an oak staff
oak_staff.description: A gnarled staff of oak, faintly warm to the touch.

wooden_shield.name: a wooden shield
wooden_shield.description: A round shield banded in iron, scarred from old fights.

leather_armor.name: a suit of leather armor
leather_armor.description: Worn but sturdy leather armor, smelling faintly of tallow.

chainmail.name: a shirt of chainmail
chainmail.description: Interlocking steel rings, heavy but reliable.

healing_potion.name: a healing potion
healing_potion.description: A small vial of red liquid that smells faintly of crushed herbs.

iron_key.name: a tarnished iron key
iron_key.description: An old iron key, green with verdigris. It looks important.

goblin_ear.name: a severed goblin ear
goblin_ear.description: A grisly trophy, still faintly twitching.

tarnished_crown.name: a tarnished silver crown
tarnished_crown.description: A small silver crown, dented and tarnished, taken from a locked chest.

steel_longsword.name: a steel longsword
steel_longsword.description: A keep-quartermaster's blade, straight and well-oiled, stamped with the Concord's anchor sigil.

tower_shield.name: a tower shield
tower_shield.description: A heavy iron-banded shield, tall enough to crouch behind.

plate_armor.name: a suit of plate armor
plate_armor.description: Polished steel plate, the kind only a Warden's guard is issued.

warden_seal.name: a tarnished warden's seal
warden_seal.description: A wax-seal stamp bearing the Harbor Concord's anchor -- it has no business being on a renegade guard.

fishhook_knife.name: a fishhook knife
fishhook_knife.description: A short, curved gutting blade, more tool than weapon, but it'll do in a pinch.

ships_log.name: a water-stained ship's log
ships_log.description: A logbook gone soft with damp. The last entries describe cargo that was never declared to the harbormaster.

stolen_ledger.name: a stolen ledger
stolen_ledger.description: Names, weights, and prices in a cramped hand -- a record the Undertow would pay dearly to get back.

undertow_signet.name: an undertow signet ring
undertow_signet.description: A black iron ring etched with a wave swallowing a coin -- the mark of the dockside syndicate.

spyglass.name: a brass spyglass
spyglass.description: A dented but functional spyglass, its lens still clear.

tin_amulet.name: a cheap tin amulet
tin_amulet.description: A worthless little charm, sold to sailors who want to believe it's lucky.

vael_sigil.name: a corroded vael sigil
vael_sigil.description: A coin-sized disc of green-black metal, warm to the touch. It fits a lock somewhere in the drowned ruins.

tide_idol.name: a small tide idol
tide_idol.description: A carved figure, half-eroded, of a many-armed shape rising from waves.

drowned_crown.name: the drowned crown
drowned_crown.description: A crown of fused coral and silver, salt-crusted, heavier than it looks. Old Vael's last king wore this.

vael_trident.name: a vael war-trident
vael_trident.description: A barbed bronze trident, etched with spiraling script no living scholar can fully read.

tidewarden_heart.name: the tidewarden's heart
tidewarden_heart.description: A fist-sized lump of something between coral and crystal, still faintly pulsing with cold light.

warden_femur_blade.name: a femur blade
warden_femur_blade.description: A short sword crudely ground from bone, dark with age. It feels wrong to hold.

grave_gold.name: a handful of grave gold
grave_gold.description: Coins buried with the dead, green with tarnish and best spent far from Temple Row.

padded_tunic.name: a padded tunic
padded_tunic.description: Quilted cloth armor, light enough to wear under a cloak.

woolen_cloak.name: a woolen cloak
woolen_cloak.description: A traveler's cloak, dyed a deep harbor-grey.

soothing_tonic.name: a soothing tonic
soothing_tonic.description: A bitter apothecary's draught that settles the stomach and the nerves alike.

greater_healing_potion.name: a greater healing potion
greater_healing_potion.description: A larger, darker vial than the common healing potion -- the apothecary's strongest brew.

mana_draught.name: a mana draught
mana_draught.description: A faintly luminous blue liquid that smells of crushed quartz.

garnet_ring.name: a garnet ring
garnet_ring.description: A simple gold band set with a deep red stone, catching what little light there is.

engraved_locket.name: an engraved locket
engraved_locket.description: A locket engraved with initials that mean nothing to anyone left in Saltspire.

dried_rations.name: dried rations
dried_rations.description: Salted fish and hard biscuit, wrapped in oilcloth. Filling, if not appetizing.

fishing_net.name: a fishing net
fishing_net.description: A coil of tarred netting, mended many times over.

sentinels_badge.name: a sentinel's badge
sentinels_badge.description: A corroded badge from a watch disbanded long before anyone alive remembers why.

captains_cutlass.name: the red tide captain's cutlass
captains_cutlass.description: A curved blade with a basket hilt, still sharp despite two centuries under the salt fields.

red_tide_standard.name: a tattered red tide standard
red_tide_standard.description: A faded banner, once carried by a pirate fleet that broke itself against Saltspire's founders.

drake_scale.name: an ash-scaled drake scale
drake_scale.description: A single scale, the size of a shield, still warm and smelling faintly of cinders.

ashwing_fang.name: an ashwing fang
ashwing_fang.description: A drake's fang as long as a forearm, the edge wickedly serrated.

hag_eye.name: a hag's preserved eye
hag_eye.description: Kept in a jar of murky brine, it seems to track you even now.

fen_mothers_staff.name: the fen mother's staff
fen_mothers_staff.description: A gnarled staff wound with river reeds and small bones, still humming faintly with old magic.
